description: A reference to an open file on the filesystem. File implements Read and Write traits for I/O operations. Files are automatically closed when they go out of scope. Python equivalent: open() built-in function.

methods.name: open
methods.description: Opens a file in read-only mode. Returns an error if the file does not exist. Python equivalent: open('file.txt', 'r').

methods.name: create
methods.description: Creates a file for writing, truncating it if it already exists. Python equivalent: open('file.txt', 'w').

methods.name: read_to_string
methods.description: A convenience function on std::fs (not File) that reads the entire contents of a file into a String. Python equivalent: Path('file.txt').read_text().

methods.name: write
methods.signature: fn write<P: AsRef<Path>, C: AsRef<[u8]>>(path: P, contents: C) -> Result<()>
methods.description: A convenience function on std::fs that writes bytes to a file, creating or overwriting it. Python equivalent: Path('file.txt').write_text().

methods.name: metadata
methods.description: Queries metadata about the file such as size, permissions, and modification time. Python equivalent: os.stat().

methods.name: write_all
methods.description: Writes the entire buffer to the file (from the Write trait). Ensures all bytes are written.
